name: BSI C3A Cloud Computing Autonomy
description: Criteria enabling Cloud Computing Autonomy (C3A) by the German Federal Office for Information Security (BSI). These criteria are based on the EU Cloud Sovereignty Framework and C5:2026.

categories.name: SOV-1 Strategic Sovereignty
categories.description: BSI Cloud Computing Autonomy criteria - SOV-1 Strategic Sovereignty

controls.name: Jurisdiction
controls.description: If restrictions are imposed by cloud service customers for Germany, they should be justified, and it should  be ensured that their implementation, for example, in procurement procedures, is legally permissible on the  basis of reasons such as public safety.  2.1.2

The cloud service provider MUST operate under EU jurisdiction, with contract governance and dispute 
resolution. 
SOV-1-01-C2 Criterion 
The cloud service provider MUST operate under German jurisdicti

The cloud service provider MUST operate under German jurisdiction, with contract governance and dispute 
resolution. 
SOV-1-01-SI Supplementary information

controls.name: Registered Office
controls.description: If the cloud service provider uses a subcontractor for the provision of services, such as operations, that  subcontractor MUST also meet the criteria.  If restrictions are imposed by cloud service customers for EU or Germany, they should be justified, and it  should be ensured that their implementation, for example, in procurement procedures, is legally  permissible on the basis of reasons such as public safety.  2.1.3

The cloud service provider MUST have a registered head office in the EU. 
SOV-1-02-C2 Criterion 
The cloud service provider MUST have a registered head office in Germany. 
SOV-1-02-SI Supplementary in

The cloud service provider MUST have a registered head office in Germany. 
SOV-1-02-SI Supplementary information

controls.name: CSP Effective Control
controls.description: Effective control in this context means the possibility to exert direct or indirect influence, or determine the  key strategic, financial, or operational decisions from non-EU undertakings.  Criteria, Additional Criteria and Supplementary Information  Federal Office for Information Security 8  If restrictions are imposed by cloud service customers for EU or Germany, they should be justified, and it  should be ensured that their implementation, for example, in procurement procedures, is legally  permissible on the basis of reasons such as public safety.  2.1.4

The cloud service provider MUST be effectively controlled by one or more EU corporations. The cloud 
service provider MUST ensure that effective controls are transparent to cloud service customers. 
S

The cloud service provider MUST be under the effective control of one or more German undertakings. The 
cloud service provider MUST ensure that effective controls are transparent to cloud service cust

controls.name: CSP Control Change
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-1-04 - CSP Control Change

The cloud service provider MUST inform cloud service customers 90 days in advance of any actual changes 
affecting the cloud service provider's control that could undermine or affect the C3A controls

categories.name: SOV-2 Legal & Jurisdictional Sovereignty
categories.description: BSI Cloud Computing Autonomy criteria - SOV-2 Legal & Jurisdictional Sovereignty

controls.name: Extraterritorial Exposure
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-2-01 - Extraterritorial Exposure

The cloud service provider MUST identify, at least once a year, any non-EU law relating directly to the 
provided cloud services that have cross-border implications for the availability of cloud servi

controls.name: Audit Rights
controls.description: The audit rights may be derived from a contract or law that explicitly reserves the right for the federal or  national authority to conduct audits. If possible, the authority tries to make use of existing audits (e.g., BSI  C5, SOC 2 Type 2) before carrying out an audit.  Any audit shall be conducted in accordance with the cloud service provider's strict security and  confidentiality protocols, including defined notice periods, to protect the data of other tenants and the  integrity of the data centre. While costs are a commercial matter, the right to audit is a regulatory mandate.  Fees shall not be so high as to effectively deny this right.  2.2.3

The cloud service provider MUST document procedures that allow the relevant federal or national 
cybersecurity authority to verify compliance with the C3A criteria by an audit. The responsible authori

The cloud service provider MUST document procedures that allow the German federal administration to 
verify compliance with the C3A criteria by an audit. 
SOV-2-02-SI Supplementary information 
The au

controls.name: State of Defense Takeover
controls.description: This usually means that the cloud service provider has the documentation, source code and administration  tools in a portable format so that a federal administration can use them.  2.3

If an EU member state declares a state of defense, the cloud service provider MUST enable the EU member 
state to take over the capabilities required to operate the cloud, including the necessary phys

If Germany declares a state of defense, the cloud service provider MUST enable the German federal 
administration to take over the capabilities required to operate the cloud, including the necessary m

categories.name: SOV-3 Data Sovereignty
categories.description: BSI Cloud Computing Autonomy criteria - SOV-3 Data Sovereignty

controls.name: Data Residence
controls.description: If a cloud service provider operates in other locations/regions in addition to the EU or Germany, the  location/region of processing and storing MUST be clearly identifiable for the cloud service customer.  2.3.2

A cloud service customer MUST be able to check where their cloud service derived data, cloud service 
customer data, and account data are stored and processed. 
SOV-3-01-C2 Criterion 
The cloud servic

The cloud service provider MUST provide a service option where cloud service derived data and account 
data are exclusively stored and processed in the EU. 
SOV-3-01-C3 Criterion 
The cloud service pr

The cloud service provider MUST provide a service option where cloud service customer data is exclusively 
stored and processed in the EU. 
SOV-3-01-C4 Criterion 
The cloud service provider MUST provi

The cloud service provider MUST provide a service option where cloud service customer data is exclusively 
stored and processed in Germany. 
SOV-3-01-C5 Criterion 
The cloud service provider MUST prov

The cloud service provider MUST provide a service option where cloud service provider data is exclusively 
stored and processed in the EU.

If a cloud service pr

controls.name: External Key Management
controls.description: The integration of external key management systems for IaaS and PaaS is widely implemented and  commonly standardized. For SaaS solutions, external encryption key management systems are less  common; therefore, cloud service providers should support external encryption key management  capabilities for SaaS where technically feasible and appropriate to the service architecture. If this criterion is  only fulfilled for some SaaS, the cloud service provider MUST provide a list of these services to the cloud  services customer.  2.3.3

The cloud service provider MUST allow the integration of external encryption key management system for 
creating, managing, and storing encryption keys outside of the cloud service provider environmen
SOV-3-02-AC Additional criterion

controls.name: External Identity Provider
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-3-03 - External Identity Provider

The cloud service provider MUST support standards-based integration of external identity providers for 
authentication and access management for the cloud service.

SOV-3-03-AC1 Additional criterion

controls.name: Logging and Monitoring
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-3-04 - Logging and Monitoring

SOV-3-04-AC1 Additional criterion
SOV-3-04-AC2 Additional criterion

controls.name: Client-Side Encryption
controls.description: If this criterion is only fulfilled for some cloud services, the cloud service provider MUST provide a list of  these services to the cloud services customer.  2.4

The cloud service provider MUST enable client-side encryption of cloud service customer data. Whenever 
the cloud service customer data is transmitted, processed or stored inside the cloud environment

categories.name: SOV-4 Operational Sovereignty
categories.description: BSI Cloud Computing Autonomy criteria - SOV-4 Operational Sovereignty

controls.name: Operating Personnel
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-4-01 - Operating Personnel

All personnel who have logical or physical access to infrastructure used to operate the cloud service, as well 
as those who are responsible for customer support, and all persons who have management c

All personnel who have logical or physical access to infrastructure used to operate the cloud service, as well 
as those who are responsible for customer support, and all persons who have management c

criterion 
The operating personnel is part of an organization that MUST be a standalone European organization.

controls.name: Redundant connectivity providers
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-4-03 - Redundant connectivity providers

The cloud service provider MUST ensure redundant and independent connectivity for the delivery of the 
sovereign cloud service. In the event of a disruption of one connectivity provider, alternative c
SOV-4-03-AC Additional criterion

controls.name: SOC
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-4-04 - SOC

cloud services are established and operated within the EU. In the case of a disconnect (SOV-4

The cloud service provider MUST ensure that Security Operations Center (SOC) capabilities for the offered 
cloud services are established and operated within Germany. In the case of a disconnect (SOV-

controls.name: Ingress Data Control
controls.description: A vulnerability is regarded as known, when it is listed in the European Union Vulnerability Database (EUVD)  or in the Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) Program from the National Institute of Standards  and Technology (NIST).  2.4.6

All software updates and operational data affecting the cloud service MUST be received, authorized and 
validated in a secured network area managed and controlled by the cloud service provider.

controls.name: Data exchange monitoring
controls.description: In the context of this requirement, a cloud service customer is not considered a third party. An associated  company within the same group of companies is classified as a third party.  2.4.8

Any cloud service derived data, cloud service customer data and account data exchanged between the cloud 
service provider and third parties MUST always be monitored, controlled and logged. In order t

controls.description: In the context of this requirement, a cloud service customer is not considered a third party. An associated  company within the same group of companies is classified as a third party.  2.4.9

The cloud service provider MUST document, define, and visualize (via a Data Flow Diagram) all data

SOV-4-08-AC Additional criterion

controls.name: Disconnect
controls.description: In the context of the disconnect requirement, network connections between the cloud service provider and  cloud service customers are excluded from the scope of the disconnection capability.  2.4.10

The cloud service provider MUST be able to disconnect all non-EU network-connections to the cloud 
without an impairment of the availability, integrity, authenticity and confidentiality of the cloud s
SOV-4-09-AC Additional criterion

controls.name: Reconnect
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-4-10 - Reconnect

The cloud service provider MUST be able to reestablish all non-EU-connections after a disconnect in 
accordance of criterion SOV-4-9-C ("Disconnect") has been performed and has a process to install up

categories.name: SOV-5 Supply Chain Sovereignty
categories.description: BSI Cloud Computing Autonomy criteria - SOV-5 Supply Chain Sovereignty

controls.name: Software Dependencies
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-5-01 - Software Dependencies

The cloud service provider MUST identify, for each cloud service, the software components used and their 
respective countries of origin. A list of the relevant software suppliers and their country or
SOV-5-01-AC Additional criterion

controls.name: Hardware Dependencies
controls.description: It is acceptable that this is only made available to the customer if he has agreed to keep the information  confidential and not publicly disclose it.  2.5.3

The cloud service provider MUST maintain a documented inventory of the hardware components used to 
provide cloud services. A list of the relevant hardware suppliers and their country or countries MUS
SOV-5-02-AC Additional criterion

controls.name: External Service Dependencies
controls.description: External services refer exclusively to services that are functionally required for the provision of the cloud  service itself. In this context, external cloud services refer to services provided by third-party cloud  providers that are integrated into the delivery of the primary cloud service but are operated and maintained  by separate providers.  It is acceptable that this is only made available to the customer if he has agreed to keep the information  confidential and not publicly disclose it.  2.5.4

The cloud service provider MUST maintain a documented inventory of used external cloud services that are 
necessary for the delivery of the cloud service. The list of information regarding the relevan
SOV-5-03-AC Additional criterion

controls.name: Export Restriction
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-5-04 - Export Restriction

The cloud service provider MUST maintain documented processes for identifying and mitigating risks 
related to export restrictions or supply chain disruptions affecting software, external services, an

controls.name: Capacity Management
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-5-05 - Capacity Management

Capacity management MUST be performed in the EU in accordance with C5. 
SOV-5-05-C2 Criterion 
Capacity management MUST be performed in Germany in accordance with C5.

Capacity management MUST be performed in Germany in accordance with C5.

categories.description: BSI Cloud Computing Autonomy criteria - SOV-6 Technology Sovereignty

controls.name: Source Code Availability
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-6-01 - Source Code Availability

The cloud service provider MUST have a backup of the source code in the EU that is not older than 24 hours 
and contains at minimum 5 versions of the cloud services so that the operation of the cloud

controls.name: Continuous Service Delivery
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-6-02 - Continuous Service Delivery

contingency strategies ensuring continued secure delivery of the cloud services. These strategies m
SOV-6-02-AC Additional criterion

controls.name: Software Development
controls.description: BSI C3A: SOV-6-03 - Software Development

The cloud service provider MUST ensure that authorised personnel have access to the software 
development tools and environments necessary to maintain and update the cloud services.
